title: melaleuca subtrigona
intro: Melaleuca subtrigona, also known as the lemon myrtle, is a plant species native to Australia, characterized by its distinctive lemon-scented leaves and aromatic essential oil. The medicinal properties of Melaleuca subtrigona include its antimicrobial and antifungal capabilities, making it effective in treating wounds and infections. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, fighting off respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, and providing antioxidant properties that may help protect against cell damage. Melaleuca subtrigona can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including essential oils, teas, and extracts, which can be used topically or taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
